notice: GENERATED CODE - DO NOT MODIFY BY HAND.

feature.name: Host Event Manage
feature.description: Orchestrates the Host Event Manage workspace and its owner-edit projection without duplicating the owning screen, component, data, or Widgetbook registries.

actionScope.included: Primary event lifecycle action rows represented by HostEventManageActionIntent.
excluded: Section selection, private and named invite-link actions, participant lifecycle mutations, report exports, and Event Success actions retain their existing owners and verification paths.

actions.description: Navigates the host to the single edit route for the managed event.
Cancels the managed event once after host confirmation.
Deletes an unused managed event once after host confirmation.

evidenceExceptions.reason: These registered Host Manage states have deterministic capture and Widgetbook proof but do not yet name focused automated test evidence in the screen contract.

actionScope.included: Edit-route recovery; schedule, location, pace, admission, cohort-cap, dynamic-pricing, and cancellation-policy decisions; event update submission; and the success return to the caller.
excluded: Raw text-field edits are form data governed by CatchField, form validators, and event data contracts rather than separately cardinalized actions.
Location search, pin placement, and confirmation belong to the Location Map child authority; this surface owns only opening that child route and consuming its result.

Generic back actions on not-found and unauthorized error scaffolds retain the shared error component's ownership; successful save feedback and caller pop are lifecycle effects rather than user actions.

actions.description: Invalidates the organizer and event providers to retry a failed owner-edit route.
Selects an editable event date and recomputes start-time validation.
Selects an editable start time and recomputes schedule validation.
Changes the event duration within the bounded stepper range.
Opens the Location Map child route and consumes its selected coordinate and place metadata.
Selects one pace from the event format's finite pace domain.
Selects one admission preset and resets incompatible cohort or pricing choices.
Enables or disables fixed cohort caps while the event policy is editable.
Enables or disables dynamic pricing and seeds its default amounts when first enabled.
Selects one cancellation policy from the finite supported policy domain.
Validates the editable form, updates the event and optional private-access policy, invalidates event readers, and returns to the caller after success.

evidenceExceptions.reason: The successful update and caller-pop transition has focused test evidence but no stable image capture because the route is intentionally dismissed.
These deterministic owner-edit states have registered capture and Widgetbook evidence but no focused state-level automated test recorded by the screen authority yet.
